data.title: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Stock Tops the S&P 500 Index in February
data.description:
Despite ongoing volatility, the stock of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) was the top-performing security in the benchmark S&P 500 index during February.

SMCI stock gained 43% over the past month, with about half that gain coming after the company filed updated and audited financial results with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) ahead of a deadline that could have seen its stock delisted from the Nasdaq exchange on which it trades.

Super Micro Computer’s stock also rallied during February as investors continue to see the company as a leading beneficiary of the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution that’s taking place. However, while the rally over the past month was impressive, SMCI stock is still down 53% over the last year.

Financial Concerns

While it sits atop the S&P 500 index today, SMCI stock was brutalized last autumn and leading into this year as concerns were raised about the company’s finances and internal accounting controls. Last year, Super Micro delayed filing its annual report with the SEC, lost its auditor Ernst & Young over governance issues, and was accused of accounting malfeasance by a notorious short seller.

Super Micro Computer was removed from the Nasdaq Composite index once before, in 2018, and subsequently listed again in January 2020. In a note to investors at the beginning of February, the company vowed not to make the same mistake twice. Super Micro Computer is expected to benefit from the AI infrastructure buildout as its servers run Nvidia (NVDA) microchips and processors.

Is SMCI Stock a Buy?

The stock of Super Micro Computer has a consensus Hold rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on three Buy, four Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. TheÂ average SMCI price targetÂ of $45.75 implies 11.83% downside risk from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on SMCI stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: American Airlines (AAL) Trials Free Inflight WiFi to Lure Travelers
data.description:
American Airlines (AAL) is testing free inflight Wi-Fi in an effort to attract more travelers and boost its ticket sales.

In a statement, American Airlines said that it will trial the complimentary WiFi on three routes in coming weeks: between Charlotte and Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina; Charlotte and Jacksonville, Florida; and on flights between Miami, Florida and Chicago, Illinois.

The airlines emphasized that the free WiFi is only in the testing phase right now and did not say when or if it will expand the complimentary service to wider parts of its network. American Airlines is the largest carrier in the world with routes to more than 350 destinations in 48 countries globally.

Freebies

American Airlines is the latest U.S. carrier to offer free services to customers on its flights, including complimentary internet access. Most of the major U.S. airlines have used freebies to draw customers back after the Covid-19 pandemic when aircraft were grounded and airlines were forced to operate flights at reduced capacity.

Delta Air Lines (DAL) and other U.S. carriers offer free Wi-Fi to members of their top tier loyalty rewards programs, though they do not make it available to all customers. Most of the complimentary inflight Wi-Fi is provided using Elon Muskâs Starlink satellite service.

American Airlines is also looking for new ways to lure customers after a business travel sales promotion failed last year. AAL stock has declined 8% in the last 12 months.

Is AAL Stock a Buy?

The stock of American Airlines has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 17 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and six Hold recommendations assigned in the past three months. The average AAL price target of $22.19 implies 54.31% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on AAL stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Stock Closes Out Its Second-Worst Month Ever
data.description:
Electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA) is closing out its second-worst month ever this February.

Shares of Tesla are up 3% on Feb. 28, trying to end a six-day losing skid. However, the current increase can’t erase the dismal performance of TSLA stock. The company’s share price is down nearly 30% over the past month, having declined in 14 of the 19 trading days during February.

The only worse month for Tesla’s stock occurred in December 2022, when the share price fell 37% amid a bear market that was created when central banks around the world began raising interest rates to dampen inflation. TSLA stock has now erased nearly all of the gains it achieved after Donald Trump was re-elected U.S. president last November.

Looking for Direction

The December 2022 plunge in TSLA stock also coincided with CEO Elon Musk finalizing his acquisition of social-media platform Twitter, which he has since renamed X. Investors were worried that Musk would become distracted with Twitter and that it could hurt Teslaâs performance.

This time around, investors are apparently worried that Musk is spending too much time in Washington, D.C. and focused on the Trump administration rather than working at Tesla. Analysts say that TSLA stock tends to fall anytime Musk appears distracted and unfocused on the automaker.

In recent weeks, TSLA stock has also been hit by weak sales figures, poor financial results, and a massive new safety recall. It has all conspired to push Tesla’s share price sharply lower throughout February. Tesla’s stock is now down 16% over the past three months.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

Tesla stock currently has a consensus Hold rating among 35 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, 12 Hold, and 10 Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. TheÂ average TSLA price targetÂ of $351.38 implies 22.10% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on TSLA stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Rebounds from Post-Earnings Selloff, Retakes $3 Trillion Market Cap
data.description:
Nvidia’s (NVDA) stock is quickly rebounding from its steep post-earnings selloff and has recaptured its $3 trillion market capitalization.

Many analysts and investors were bracing for the worst after NVDA stock fell 8.5% on Feb. 27, the day after it delivered its latest quarterly financial results. The sharp one-day decline wiped nearly $275 billion from the company’s value and pushed its market cap down to $2.94 trillion.

However, after initially falling further in early trading on Feb. 28, the last trading day of the month, NVDA stock has rebounded and is up 2% midday, pushing its market cap back to $3 trillion. Apple (AAPL) is the only other publicly traded company with a market capitalization above $3 trillion.

Perception Problem?

Some analysts expressed surprise at the selloff in Nvidiaâs stock, coming as it did after the chipmaker reported financial results that beat Wall Street forecasts across the board, including a 78% year-over-year rise in its sales and an 80% increase in its profits.

The companyâs data center revenue, which includes its microchips that power artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, soared 93% on an annual basis to nearly $36 billion. Nvidia also signaled that production issues for its next-generation Blackwell microchips have been resolved.

Analysts attributed the immediate post-earnings selloff to investor concerns about the potential impacts of export controls, tariffs, and more efficient AI models out of China. Despite the recent drawdown, Nvidia is still worth five times more than it was two years ago at the start of the AI boom. The companyâs market capitalization first hit $3 trillion in June 2024.

NVDA stock is currently down 9% on the year.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidiaâs stock currently has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 38 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average NVDA price target of $178.66 implies 45.89% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on NVDA stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Nvidia’s (NVDA) Pullback May Be an Overreaction
data.description:
Nvidia’s (NVDA) share price has struggled so far in 2025 following the emergence of DeepSeek AI and a mixed near-term outlook. Nevertheless, some believe that the chipmaker’s recent pullback is an overreaction due to a crucial but overlooked factor – the long-term profit potential of sovereign artificial intelligence. Indeed, nation-states have begun investing heavily in AI infrastructure, which will inevitably drive demand for high-powered chips made by Nvidia.

As Anjney Midha, a partner at a16z generator, noted on Yahoo Finance’s Opening Bid podcast, these infrastructure build-outs are just beginning. It is worth noting that the scale of these investments is massive. In fact, the European Union has set aside 200 billion euros for AI investments, while Microsoft-backed OpenAI (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), and Softbank (SFTBY) have pledged up to $500 billion for an AI infrastructure project.

Unsurprisingly, Nvidia’s AI chips will be at the core of these build-outs, with countries like Canada and Denmark already announcing that they will use the company’s products to develop their AI infrastructure. As a result, while Nvidia’s near-term outlook may be mixed, the company’s long-term outlook remains bright, with CEO Jensen Huang and CFO Colette Kress highlighting the strong demand for Nvidia’s chips and the potential for improved margins later in the year.

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, analysts remain bullish on NVDA stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months. AfterÂ a 54% rally in its share priceÂ over the past year, theÂ average NVDA price target of $178.66 per shareÂ implies an upside potential of 46.7% from current levels.

See more NVDA analyst ratings
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Announces Layoffs in Its Cloud Unit
data.description:
Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) has announced a new round of layoffs in its cloud computing and human resources units.

The Silicon Valley-based company says that the latest cuts are being undertaken as part of an internal reorganization. The exact number of people being let go isn’t known, but the layoffs are scheduled to occur in early March of this year.

Senior executives at Alphabet have said they plan to implement staff cuts throughout 2025 as they look to raise money that can be invested in the company’s artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure. Alphabet has announced plans to spend $75 billion on its AI buildout this year.

Ongoing Cuts

The cuts to Alphabet’s cloud team are expected to mostly impact support staff and positions outside the U.S. Impacted employees will receive severance equivalent to 14 weeks salary and one additional week for every full year of service. Cloud computing continues to be one of the companyâs high-growth areas and is benefitting from AI-integration within various products.

Earlier this year, Alphabet announced layoffs and offered buyout packages to staff in the companyâs Platforms and Devices unit. That unit houses more than 25,000 employees who work on Android, Chrome, Pixel, Fitbit and Nest devices. Since 2022, Alphabet has cut more than 12,000 jobs following a hiring spree during the Covid-19 pandemic.

GOOGL stock has risen 22% in the last 12 months.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 37 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 27 Buy and 10 Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $215.85 implies 28.41% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on GOOGL stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Palantir’s Stock (PLTR) Plunge Has Led to a Drop in Retail Investor Purchases
data.description:
Palantir’s stock (PLTR) price has plummeted 32% since hitting a record high on February 18. This downturn has led to a significant drop in purchases from retail investors, according to data from Vanda Research. Interestingly, retail investors have been one of the key drivers of the AI software company’s stock performance, so their declining optimism could be a major blow to bullish investors.

One major factor that has contributed to the sell-off is the planned sale of up to 9.975 million shares by CEO Alex Karp. This move has caused investors to worry that the stock may be overvalued, especially after its 248% run-up over the past year. It also does not help that other Palantir insiders have been aggressively selling their shares. Furthermore, it is worth noting that the company’s reliance on government contracts makes it vulnerable to potential defense spending cuts, which could further impact its stock price.

Another issue that investors should be aware of is the fact that the company’s top three customers account for a significant portion of its revenue. Therefore, any disruption in their operations could have a material impact on Palantir’s revenue. Given these challenges, five-star Jefferies analyst Brent Thill suggested that Palantir’s stock price may be due for a correction, especially when considering its high valuation and potential headwinds. In fact, Thill has a Sell rating on PLTR with a $60 price target.

What Is the Prediction for PLTR Stock?

Overall, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PLTR stock based on four Buys, nine Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, theÂ average PLTR price target of $93.13 per shareÂ implies 11.4% upside potential.Â

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Robinhood Stock Jumps as Uniswap Deal Boosts Crypto Access
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD) stock surged 2.4% at the start of regular trading hours today as investors reacted to its latest moveâpartnering with Uniswap, MoonPay, and Transak to simplify crypto-to-cash conversions.

Uniswapâs New Partnership Makes Crypto Cashouts Seamless

Uniswap, the worldâs largest decentralized exchange, has teamed up with Robinhood, MoonPay, and Transak to let users sell their crypto directly for fiat. According to Uniswapâs announcement, this new feature is now live in over 180 countries through Uniswapâs mobile wallet and will soon be available on its web app and browser extension.

SEC Closes Investigations into Uniswap and Robinhood

The new partnership comes just days after the SEC dropped its investigations into both Uniswap and Robinhoodâs crypto division. The agency had been scrutinizing Robinhoodâs crypto operations but ultimately decided not to take enforcement action. Similarly, Uniswap had been under regulatory pressure since receiving a Wells notice in April 2023. With these legal uncertainties lifted, both companies now have room to expand their crypto services without fear of immediate regulatory roadblocks.

UNI Token Drops Despite Positive News

While Robinhood stock saw a boost, Uniswapâs UNI (UNI-USD) token fell 5.4% to $7.60, weighed down by a broader crypto market slump. However, the long-term impact of this partnership could strengthen Uniswapâs position in decentralized finance.

Is HOOD Stock a Good Buy?

Analysts are cautiously bullish about HOOD stock, with a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and 5 Holds. Over the past year, HOOD has increased byÂ more than 210%, and theÂ average HOOD price targetÂ of $71.27 implies an upside potential of 42.6% from current levels.

See more HOOD analyst ratings
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Moves to Facial Recognition with New iPhone Model
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is moving to facial recognition with its new more affordable iPhone model.

The iPhone 16e, which begins shipping today (Feb. 28), is adding face identification as its main biometric security feature, replacing the fingerprint technology or “Touch ID” that has long been the primary way for users to access the smartphones.

Appleâs new iPhone is aimed at more budget-conscious consumers and is the first smartphone from the consumer electronics giant to focus on Face ID to unlock the device rather than a fingerprint scan.

âItâs the most effortless way of authenticating,â said Joe Palmer, chief innovation officer at iProov, a global technology company focused on biometric verification and authentication. If you think about how many times you unlock a phone in a day, even if it takes you a second and youâre unlocking the phone 100 times a day, it adds up, he said. âI donât think weâre going to see an evolution beyond face anytime soon,â he added.

Still, technology and cybersecurity professionals say fingerprint scanning technology has plenty of life left â and Apple itself is likely to offer the option in future device releases, including smartphones.

Hereâs what consumers need to understand about the latest biometric trends in smartphones, and whatâs likely to come next:

Why fingerprints could still make a comeback
Appleâs Touch ID continues to be available in certain iPad models, and the company is likely to reintroduce the technology in subsequent versions of its smartphones, according to experts consulted by CNBC. One sign they point to that makes this likely: The company was granted a patent several years ago for under-display fingerprint reading technology and continues to work on improvements, according to several published reports. As a result, the company is likely to bring back Touch ID to smartphones once it perfects its version of under-the-screen technology.

Apple declined to comment.

Consumers like choices, Palmer said, offering the example of a colleague who uses facial authentication to unlock an Android phone and fingerprint technology to authorize payments. Once Apple introduces fingerprint technology under the screen, it will likely be available in flagship phones again and work its way down through the models, he said.

Appleâs near-term move away from fingerprint technology in its smartphones makes sense for several reasons. For one, the company has always had a larger facial recognition culture, in part because its technology is solid and easy to use, said Roger Grimes, an analyst at KnowBe4, a security platform provider.

Itâs designed to automatically adapt to changes in user appearance, such as wearing cosmetic makeup or growing facial hair. Itâs also designed to work with hats, scarves, glasses, contact lenses and many sunglasses. The company designed the technology to work indoors, outdoors, and even in total darkness. With iPhone 12 or later, Face ID also works with face masks.

The move away from Touch ID on smartphones is also an attempt to appeal to customers who want more screen space on their devices, technology professionals said. In past phone versions, Appleâs Touch ID fingerprint sensor was integrated into a phoneâs home or power button. Whereas the iPhone 16e â similar to the iPhone 10 â has a notch, a physical area on its display for sensors. This design element has been used in smartphones for several years to accommodate front-facing cameras and microphones while meeting consumer demand for larger edge-to-edge screens. âApple has been slowly trying to remove the home button from phones for many years to get the edge-to-edge experience where the entire phone is a screen and thereâs no wasted space,â Palmer said.

Thumb tech is cost-effective
Fingerprint technology continues to be available on Android devices, and thatâs not likely to change anytime soon, even as newer phones offer facial authentication as an option, said Jean Fang, senior consultant for biometrics and authentication at Fime, which offers consulting and testing services to the payments industry.

Face Unlock is available on Pixel 4 and Pixel 7 or later Pixel phones, including Pixel Fold, according to Googleâs website. On Pixel 8 and later, consumers can use Face Unlock to verify their identity when they sign into apps or approve a purchase. The face recognition feature can be used on Galaxy phones or tablets to unlock the device and verify the userâs identity in certain apps, according to Samsungâs website.

Even as more devices adopt facial authentication, fingerprint technology will remain a solid option for many phone users, technology professionals said. For one thing, fingerprint scanning is more cost-effective than other options such as iris or palm scans. âItâs a very good technology and itâs very mature and we have fingerprint sensors that are affordable everywhere,â said D. J. Lee, a professor in the department of electrical and computer engineering at Brigham Young University.

âIt works the way we need it to work most of the time,â said Grimes.

Biometric security limitations
To be sure, there are downsides to popular biometric options. Fingerprint authentication doesnât always work properly, if, for example, a personâs finger is wet or chapped, or the sensor canât detect an exact match for another reason. But facial authentication technology also has drawbacks, especially as deepfake technology advances, said Fang, who is also a member of the Secure Technology Alliance, a not-for-profit, multi-industry association focused on identity, access and payments. There can also be limitations on how well facial authentication works depending on factors such as lighting and whether the person had facial surgery such as a nose job or eyebrow lift, she said.

âIt can be a good feature for some lower-risk cases, but not all cases,â Fang said.

Despite the limitations of existing biometric modules, fingerprint and face authentication technology are expected to be the go-to biometric methods for the foreseeable future. Thatâs not for lack of testing of other methods, but for more practical reasons. About 15 years ago, Grimes participated in a product test that tried to identify users by smell, which seemed to work well until the test subjects ate a lot of garlic or drank alcohol. âIt turned out a lot of people really liked garlic and that would overwhelm their scent and you have a lot of people that drink a lot,â he said.

While itâs possible to authenticate users through other biometric methods, like iris or palm scans â Amazon Whole Foodsâ stores palm payments tech being a recent example â in many cases these may cost more and add more friction for users, making widespread adoption less likely. âItâs the balance between security, the convenience and the cost,â Lee said.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 33 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy, 11 Hold, and four Sell recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $250.95 implies 1.02% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on AAPL stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump’s Tax-Free Crypto Plan Could Backfire and Wreak Havoc
data.description:
Trumpâs rumored plan to eliminate capital gains tax on U.S.-based cryptocurrencies might sound like a win for investors, but experts warn it could destabilize global crypto markets.

Trump’s Plan Could Happen Immediately

If Trumpâs administration follows through on the reported tax exemption, U.S. investors could dump non-U.S. cryptos to rotate into tax-free domestic options. This shift could increase sell pressure on global projects, particularly those heavily reliant on U.S. investors. As cited by CoinDesk, analysts warn that this sudden change could trigger price volatility across the board.

A Flood of New Coins Could Cause Scams

Zero capital gains tax might fuel a crypto gold rush in the U.S., much like the 2017 ICO boom, which saw nearly 80% of projects collapse or turn into scams. If the tax break arrives before clear regulations, bad actors could take advantage of inexperienced investors, damaging cryptoâs reputation. The FBI has already warned about fake tokens preying on unsuspecting buyers.

Could This Hurt the Global Crypto Industry?

With U.S. venture capital firms favoring local tokens to maximize tax-free gains, global projects may struggle to secure funding. Countries like the UAE have tax-free crypto policies, but they apply them universallyâunlike Trumpâs proposed U.S.-only plan. According to CoinDesk, this could fragment liquidity, reduce competition, and isolate American investors from the broader crypto economy. It’s also important to note that while tax relief may sound attractive, it risks fueling scams, weakening global markets, and stifling innovation.

It is important to see how these developments impact crypto prices. Therefore, investors should stay ahead by tracking well-established cryptos on TipRanksâ Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani
